mission.title: traefik: Support Failover Load-balancing
mission.description: Support Failover Load-balancing. Requested by 40+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
### What version of Traefik are you using (`traefik version`)?
v1.1.2

### What is your environment & configuration (arguments, toml...)?
linux, simple file backend

```toml
[backends]
  [backends.testing]
    [backends.testing.servers.server1]

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Failover Load-balancing
steps.description:
### What does this PR do?

This pull request adds a Failover service type. This service allows forwarding the requests sent to the `main` service to the `fallback` service when the `main` service is not available anymore (failing health checks).

### Motivation

Fix #1007

### More

- [X] Added/updated tests
- [X] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

Co-authored-by: Kevin Pollet <pollet.kevin.com>

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support Failover Load-balancing" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### What does this PR do?

This pull request adds a Failover service type. This service allows forwarding the requests sent to the `main` service to the `fallback` service when the `main` service is not available anymore (failing health checks).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
